requirementCueLines: Business Analytics & AI Concentration - School of Business
Business Analytics & AI Concentration

New in autumn 2026 , the Business Analytics & AI Concentration prepares students with practical analytics and AI skills that are in high demand and essential across all modern business environments. This concentration will prepare students to be data-driven thinkers who can bridge business and technology and help drive innovation across a wide range of business functions. Increasing industry demand for graduates who can work effectively with data and AI-driven tools is addressed through this practical and forward-looking curriculum that emphasizes not only AI fluency but also critical thinking, judgement and responsibility.
This concentration requires students to complete 90 total credits of coursework. The table below outlines the course requirements and elective options for this concentration.
BBUS 300 – Organizational Behavior, Ethics and Inclusivity, Diversity Designated Course
BBUS 307 – Business Writing, Writing Designated Course
BBUS 310 – Managerial Economics (BMATH 144, or STMATH 124, or MATH 112)
BBUS 320 – Marketing Management
BBUS 340 – Operations & Project Management
BBUS 350 – Business Finance (310)
Required Courses (prerequisites in parentheses)
B BUS 309 – Quantitative Analysis for Business (Focused on Business Data Visualization)
B BUS 479 – Business Intelligence and AI
Required Electives
Choose two courses (10 credits) from the following:
B BUS 301 Data Management (( BIS 215, or BBUS 215, or B MATH 215, or STMATH 341)
B BUS 330 Information Management and Analysis
B BUS 423 Market Intelligence (320)
B BUS 429 Marketing Analytics (320)
B BUS 452 Financial Modeling and AI (350)
B BUS 489 Digital Lab ( (330; CSS 173 or CSS 143 and corequisite CSSSKL 143; CSS 360)
B BUS 468 Advanced Accounting and Analytics (363)
B BUS 472 Data Driven Managerial Problem Solving (300)
30 credits
Required Capstone Courses
Before you can register for the capstone courses, you must complete all core courses and meet with your advisor to file a graduation application .
BBUS 470 – Business Policy & Strategic Management
BBUS 480 – Global Environment of Business
Total Credits
90 Credits
